title: Move Service Endpoint URLs from Source Code to Environment Configuration
description:
Externalize all API endpoint URLs from the source code into the Laravel `.env` configuration file. This is a security and configuration best practice that ensures endpoint URLs are not hardcoded in the repository and can vary by deployment environment.

## Environment Variables Added
- `service_one_all`, `service_one_dev`, `service_one_stage`, `service_one_prod` — Metrics Service 1 (parsedashboard) API Gateway endpoints
- `service_two_all`, `service_two_dev`, `service_two_stage`, `service_two_prod` — Metrics Service 2 Lambda function URLs
- `notion_rest_api` — Notion REST API base URL
acceptance_criteria: All service endpoint URLs are removed from source code and placed in .env
Each environment (dev, stage, prod) has its own .env with correct URLs
Application reads endpoint URLs from environment configuration at runtime
No API endpoint URLs remain hardcoded in the repository
Notion REST API URL is also externalized to .env
middleware_changes: All hardcoded API endpoint URLs replaced with env() calls referencing .env configuration. Service 1, Service 2, and Notion API endpoints are now environment-driven.
implementation_notes: Follows Laravel convention of using .env for environment-specific configuration. The env vars follow a naming pattern: service_{number}_{environment}. Notion API URL was also externalized during this cleanup.
comment_summary: Balaji initially started working on the ticket. Waqar completed it and deployed to development with all service URLs and the Notion API URL externalized to .env variables.
